sections.markdown:
## EXERCISE 3: OpenPipeline 
## Please duplicate the Notebook before editing it. If you need instructions, check out the Lab Guide. Thanks!

We will leverage the DQL query provided below to report the sales amount by country and state on a continuous basis.  OpenPipeline allows us to do that.

input.value:
fetch logs

| filter k8s.deployment.name=="accounting"
| filter service.name == "accounting"
| fields timestamp,  k8s.deployment.name, k8s.namespace.name, k8s.pod.name, service.name, `@orderresult`, dt.entity.cloud_application_instance

| filter isNotNull(`@orderresult`)

| parse `@orderresult`, "JSON:json_order_result"
| fieldsAdd cost_json=json_order_result[shippingCost], adress=json_order_result[shippingAddress]
| fields timestamp, totalcost=toDouble(cost_json[units]), country=adress[country], state=adress[state]

sections.markdown:
Whenever you have a finalized DQL query, you can now more easily create an OpenPipeline configuration to continuously report the data for end-user teams.

For next steps, let's follow along in the Lab Guide for Exercise 3
